items.title: Australia’s ‘unfair’ visa price hikes: what are they, why have they gone up, and why does it matter? - The Guardian
items.commentary: Australia significantly increased visa application prices across multiple visa categories, drawing criticism that the hikes are unfair. Applicants across student, partner, work and other visa types now face substantially higher upfront costs when applying.

items.title: 'You've got no choice': The $1,475 fee leaving some in Australia feeling 'captive' - SBS
items.commentary: A visa fee of $1,475 has left some applicants feeling trapped, unable to change their situation without paying the increased charge. The cost affects people who must lodge or extend certain visas, raising the financial barrier to staying in Australia lawfully.

items.title: Family allowed into Australia to care for slain woman’s children after commissioner’s intervention - SMH.com.au
items.commentary: A family was permitted to enter Australia to care for the children of a woman who was killed, following intervention by a commissioner. The decision shows how discretionary powers can allow entry outside standard visa pathways in exceptional humanitarian circumstances.

items.title: Court told former Wagga Wagga MP was 'kept in dark' over visa scheme - ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
items.source: ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
items.commentary: A court heard that a former MP was allegedly kept uninformed about the workings of a visa scheme under scrutiny. The case highlights legal risks around fraudulent or improperly run visa arrangements affecting visa holders and applicants.

items.title: Employers face new salary floors and 25 % work-visa fee hike under 1 July overhaul - t.co
items.commentary: New salary floors and a 25 percent work-visa fee increase took effect from 1 July as part of a broader overhaul. Employers sponsoring skilled workers must now pay higher wages and higher application fees to secure and retain those visas.

items.title: Court told former MP knew lying was 'part and parcel' of visa scheme - ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
items.source: ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
items.commentary: A court was told a former MP knew that lying was 'part and parcel' of a visa scheme. The proceedings underline the legal consequences of dishonesty in visa sponsorship and application processes.

items.title: Labor’s skilled migration program exposed as less than half of ‘skilled’ visa recipients are primary workers - Sky News Australia
items.commentary: Analysis showed that fewer than half of recipients of Australia's skilled visas are primary skilled workers, with the rest being dependants. This affects how skilled migration numbers are interpreted and may influence future policy on visa allocation.

items.title: 'I'm astounded': The visa fee that's nearly tripled in price - SBS
items.commentary: A particular Australian visa fee has nearly tripled in price, surprising applicants. The sharp increase raises the cost significantly for those seeking that visa category.

items.title: The overlooked factor in Australia's migration debate that's keeping figures high - SBS
items.commentary: Reporting examined an often-overlooked factor keeping Australia's migration figures high amid ongoing debate. The discussion affects how policymakers may adjust visa settings and intake levels going forward.

items.title: Australia raises salary requirement for skilled work visas - MSN
items.commentary: Australia raised the minimum salary requirement for skilled work visas. Employers must now offer higher wages to sponsor workers, and some roles may no longer qualify under the increased threshold.

items.title: From student to partner visas: Australian visa application fees increased from July 2026 - SBS
items.commentary: Australian visa application fees increased from July 2026 across categories ranging from student to partner visas. Applicants in these streams now face higher upfront charges when lodging their applications.

items.title: 'Robbing people using visas': Applicants shocked by sudden price hike - ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
items.source: ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
items.commentary: Visa applicants expressed shock at a sudden price hike affecting application fees. The increase raises the immediate cost of applying for or renewing affected Australian visas.

items.title: Surprise hike in international student visa application fees “a direct hit to Australia’s competitiveness” - ICEF Monitor
items.commentary: International student visa application fees rose unexpectedly, with concerns it could reduce Australia's appeal to prospective students. Higher upfront fees add to the cost of studying in Australia and may influence enrolment decisions.
